title: Why 6,988 Pages of Tax Code Are Actually Written to Help You Pay Less
guestBio: Barbara Schreihans is the founder and CEO of Your Tax Coach, creator of the Write Off Your Life™ Course, and a nationally recognized tax strategist. She helps entrepreneurs and high net-worth individuals save tens of millions in taxes while growing their profits through strategic planning and proper entity structures.
excerpt: Most entrepreneurs overpay taxes because they don't understand that the tax code was written FOR them, not against them. Barbara Schreihans reveals why only 12 pages tell you what to pay while 6,988 pages show you how to pay less.
metaDescription: Learn tax strategies that save entrepreneurs thousands. Barbara Schreihans explains S-Corp benefits, deductions, and why the tax code helps you pay less.
keyTakeaways: The tax code contains 7,000 pages with only 12 telling you what to pay while 6,988 pages show you how to pay less legally.
S-Corps make you 35 times less likely to be audited than sole proprietorships and can save thousands in self-employment taxes.
Switching to an S-Corp structure can save over $6,000 annually even on just $40,000 in income.
Women have higher investment returns than men but don't invest enough due to confidence issues around money.
Your accountant should explain how to maximize deductions, not just say no to legitimate business expenses.
Starting investments with as little as $5 per week can build significant wealth over time due to compound interest.
Regular money conversations with your spouse prevent the financial conflicts that cause many divorces.

pullQuotes.text: The tax code was written for entrepreneurs. Only 12 pages tell you what to pay - the other 6,988 pages tell you how to pay less.
Your accountant should tell you HOW, not just say NO to deductions.
Women actually outperform men when they invest - we just need to invest more.

timestamps.label: Why taxes feel overwhelming for women
The tax code as roadmap, not rulebook
When to think strategically about taxes
Entity structure: LLC vs S-Corp vs C-Corp
Having money dates with your spouse
Mindset shifts around money and investing

content:
Here's what no one tells you about taxes: the system was actually designed to help entrepreneurs, not hurt them. While most business owners spend their time afraid of the IRS and overpaying taxes, smart entrepreneurs understand that the tax code is their roadmap to wealth building.

In this game-changing episode of The Manage Her, host Aimee Rickabus sits down with Barbara Schreihans, founder of Your Tax Coach and nationally recognized tax strategist, to reveal the truth about taxes that your accountant might not be telling you. Barbara's approach isn't just about saving money on April 15th - it's about fundamentally shifting how you think about money, investing, and building wealth as a female entrepreneur.

The Tax Code Was Written For You, Not Against You

Barbara drops a truth bomb that will change everything: out of 7,000 pages in the tax code, only 12 pages tell you what you owe. The remaining 6,988 pages? They're dedicated to showing you how to legally pay less. The tax code is literally a roadmap for entrepreneurs to build wealth.

This isn't about sketchy loopholes or aggressive strategies that will get you in trouble. This is about understanding that the government wants entrepreneurs to succeed, create jobs, and invest in the economy. The tax benefits you can claim aren't rewards for being sneaky - they're incentives built into the system to encourage the exact behavior that drives economic growth.

Think about it this way: when you write off your home office, you're being rewarded for creating jobs and contributing to the economy from that space. When you deduct business travel, you're being incentivized to expand your market reach and grow your business. The tax code isn't your enemy - it's your ally in building wealth.

The Entity Structure Mistake Costing You Thousands

Here's where most entrepreneurs get it wrong: they stay stuck in sole proprietorship or LLC structures long after they should have made the switch to an S-Corporation. Barbara reveals that this single mistake can cost you 15.3% in unnecessary self-employment taxes on every dollar you earn.

Even if you're only making $40,000 a year, switching to an S-Corp can save you over $6,000 annually. But here's the kicker - S-Corps also make you 35 times less likely to be audited compared to sole proprietorships. You're not just saving money, you're also reducing your risk.

The beauty of an S-Corp structure is that it allows you to split your income between salary and distributions, only paying self-employment taxes on the salary portion. This isn't tax evasion - it's tax optimization using the exact structure the IRS created for this purpose.

Stop Letting Your Accountant Say No

One of Barbara's most powerful messages is this: your accountant should tell you HOW, not just say NO. Too many entrepreneurs accept their accountant's conservative approach without question, missing out on thousands of dollars in legitimate deductions.

Barbara shares stories of clients who were told they couldn't write off their designer handbags, business travel, or even vehicles - only to discover these were completely legitimate business expenses when structured properly. The problem isn't that these deductions don't exist; it's that many accountants take the path of least resistance rather than helping you maximize your tax benefits.

Your business expenses should reflect your business activities. If you're networking at high-end events, that designer handbag isn't vanity - it's a business tool. If you're meeting clients or attending conferences, that travel isn't a vacation - it's a business investment. The key is proper documentation and understanding the business purpose behind every expense.

The Wealth-Building Strategies You're Missing

Barbara doesn't just focus on reducing your tax bill - she shows you how to use tax strategies to build real wealth. Solo 401(k)s, cash balance plans, and other retirement vehicles don't just defer taxes; they create tax-free growth that compounds over decades.

Here's what's particularly important for women: research shows that women actually achieve higher investment returns than men, but we don't invest enough. We often tell ourselves we're "bad with money" or that we need to wait until we understand everything perfectly before we start. Barbara's message is clear - start now, even with just $5 a week.

The time value of money means that starting small today beats waiting to invest large amounts later. Those small, consistent investments compound over time, and the tax-advantaged accounts available to business owners accelerate that growth exponentially.

Money Conversations That Strengthen Relationships

Barbara addresses something many female entrepreneurs struggle with - having productive money conversations with their spouses. Money is the number one cause of divorce, but it doesn't have to be a source of conflict in your relationship.

The solution? Schedule regular "money dates" where you discuss finances openly and without judgment. These aren't confrontational budget meetings - they're collaborative planning sessions where you align on goals and celebrate progress together.

The key is approaching these conversations as partners working toward shared objectives, not adversaries protecting individual interests. When both people understand the financial picture and feel heard in the planning process, money becomes a tool for building your shared future rather than a source of stress.

Your Permission Slip to Think Bigger

Perhaps the most important message from this conversation is this: you have permission to think bigger about money. The tax strategies Barbara discusses aren't just for massive corporations or ultra-wealthy individuals - they're available to any entrepreneur willing to structure their business properly and think strategically about their finances.

You don't need to wait until you're making millions to start implementing these strategies. You don't need to apologize for wanting to minimize your taxes or maximize your wealth. The system was designed to reward entrepreneurs who create value, generate jobs, and invest in growth. You're not gaming the system - you're using it exactly as intended.

Stop letting fear or confusion keep you from the financial strategies that could transform your business and your life. The tax code isn't written in a foreign language - it's written in the language of opportunity for entrepreneurs bold enough to learn it.
